carbonate_acid_gas.pedagogical_note: The intermediate is H₂CO₃, which immediately decomposes into CO₂ and H₂O.

sulfite_acid_gas.pedagogical_note: The intermediate is H₂SO₃, which decomposes into SO₂ and H₂O.

sulfide_acid_gas.pedagogical_note: H₂S is a gas with the smell of rotten eggs. Produced only from soluble sulfides.

ammonium_alkali_gas.pedagogical_note: The intermediate is NH₃·H₂O, which decomposes releasing ammonia NH₃.

h2so4_conc_oxidizer.pedagogical_note: Reduction products of S(+6): SO₂ (active metals), S or H₂S (excess metal).

hno3_oxidizer.pedagogical_note: Reduction products of N(+5): NO₂ (conc.), NO (dil.), N₂O, N₂, NH₃ (very active metals).

passivation_cold_conc.pedagogical_note: Passivation — formation of a dense oxide film preventing further reaction. Reaction proceeds when heated.

amphoteric_both_directions.pedagogical_note: Examples: Al₂O₃, ZnO, BeO, Al(OH)₃, Zn(OH)₂, Be(OH)₂.
